channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-432] Add support for Fedora14 kernel 2.6.35.13-92.fc14 (1.8.x patchless client)
item.summary: Add support for Fedora14 kernel 2.6.35.13-92.fc14 (1.8.x patchless client)

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:

Close as above comment.
I think this ticket can be closed for now. Since FC15 support will be landed sooner. It includes all the changes needed by FC14.
This seems to be landed for both 1.8.7 and 2.1. Can it be marked as resolved now or is there some additional work still outstanding?

LU-432 QUOTA_OK / NO_QUOTA definitions

Johann Lombardi : 7546c36e8f4f0fd154af9ee28123c30da8f02ad6
Files :

  • lustre/include/linux/lustre_compat25.h
  • lustre/quota/quota_interface.c

I did the change that was recommended for "LU-432 QUOTA_OK / NO_QUOTA definitions" but build fails (looks like a general issue with the building infrastructure).

Regarding the autoconf recommended changes, the proposed file to add my changes does not exist (it exists only in master branch).

Regards,
Christos

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,client,el5,inkernel #185
LU-432 multiop test needs $(PTHREAD_LIBS)

Oleg Drokin : a832ab57bda8658457193cc670b72a9995f10ff0
Files :

  • lustre/tests/Makefile.am
master patch： http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,994

Christos, thanks for submitting the patches.

Sorry to be picky about the changes, but the kernel compat checks need to be kept "clean" since the changes to the kernel API change so frequently this code would otherwise just get increasingly more difficult to maintain.

Please also submit similar patches for the "master" (2.x) branch.

Yang Sheng

Can you please review these patch contributions and see about porting them to land for master

Thanks

Peter

Hi,
it looks that all my patches but the last compiled fine at jekins [1]. I'm not sure what is the problem with the last as the change is totally trivial (adding -lpthread at compilation of a test) but for sure this is not critical for the 2.6.35 kernel support (at least i'm happy without it).

I'm not sure how i should proceed now. Do i have to do something more or is it up to you now?

Regards,
Christos

[1] http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,978

Christos

I do understand your situation. I'm afraid that running 2.x clients with 1.8.x servers is not supported, so I am afraid that your suggestion is not an option. Running old servers with new clients is usually supported between subsequent versions but as this is a major version change this is not the case. If you contribute the work against 1.8.x then an engineer can always port this work to master for landing. This should give you the feedback that you need, even if we choose not to land the patches for 1.8.x. Smaller patches definitely make things easier.

Regards

Peter

Hi Peter,
i totally understand your concern on using the master branch instead of the 1.8.x but the problem here is that our deployment is still on 1.8.4 thus i don't have the needed testing infrastructure for the master branch.

I'm not sure if building a patchless client based on master branch would work fluently with our old (1.8.4) servers. This is something that i'm willing to test on a later phase.

Anyway we are planning to update our infrastructure, hopefully in this summer, thus i'll anyway have to migrate these patches to the master branch unless someone else already have done them (as they are not fedora specific but kernel 2.6.35 specific).

Regards,
Christos

PS: I'm cleaning up my patch and splitting it to small commits to ease the landing procedure. I'll push them for review hopefully tonight.

Hi Christos

Thanks for your interest in Lustre and your willingness to contribute to its developement. I saw your discussion about this work with Whamcloud's Andreas Dilger and would further add to his comments that you should review the instructions at http://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/PUB/Submitting+Changes about the logisitcs of contributing code to Lustre. Furthermore, it would be easiest to get your work quickly landed if it was baselined on the master branch rather than 1.8.x. The master branch is the basis for the upcoming Lustre 2.1 release and all future Lustre releases.

Regards

Peter
